pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.003; Kruskal-Wallis p=0.0036; CADD 29.4 (highest of all variants); none survived Bonferroni (0.000091)
evidence_quote: The only coding variant, rs1126809:A (missense, exon 4), makes the TYR enzyme thermosensitive and less active; the rate-limiting role of TYR predicts less pigment with rs1126809:A.
author_explanation_quote: The alternative allele, rs1126809:A, introduces a missense mutation that causes the TYR enzyme to be thermosensitive, thus less active...TYR is the rate limiting enzyme in melanogenesis
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYR target region)

coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 chr9:12,698,471 (melanocyte-specific promoter, non-coding TYRP1 transcript)
pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.0055; Kruskal-Wallis p=0.035; CADD 17.66; none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: rs35866166:C (CADD 17.6) was more frequent in brown-eyed individuals and predicted to create six new TF binding sites, possibly increasing expression of a non-coding transcript with a positive effect on melanogenesis.
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; predicted 6 new TF binding sites (hedged mechanism).
author_explanation_quote: the alternative allele, rs35866166:C, was predicted to generate six additional transcription factor binding sites. It is possible that these sites lead to increased expression of the non-coding TYRP1 transcript, and that the non-coding TYRP1 transcript has a positive effect on melanogenesis
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYRP1 target region)
notes: Mechanism is explicitly hedged ('It is possible...', 'It is unknown whether the non-coding TYRP1 transcript has a role in melanogenesis') but a concrete molecular hypothesis is given, not left as bare unexplained association.

coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 chr9:12,679,244 (melanocyte-specific enhancer, ~14 kb upstream of TYRP1)
pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.0024; Kruskal-Wallis p=0.0052; CADD 3.41; none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: rs62538956:C was predicted to disrupt a YY1 (MITF-cooperating) binding site ~14 kb upstream of TYRP1; although reduced TYRP1 would predict lighter (blue) eyes, rs62538956:C was mostly in brown-eyed individuals, suggesting LD with an unidentified causal variant.
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; rs62538956 -- 'we found no explanation of the effect' -- variant-mechanism gap on a textbook effector, out of scope for effector-uncertain.
author_explanation_quote: Although we found no explanation of the effect of rs62538956:C...It is possible that rs62538956 is in LD with a causative variant, which was not identified or annotated with regulatory effects in this study
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYRP1 target region)
notes: Predicted YY1-disruption mechanism contradicts the OBSERVED direction of effect; authors explicitly state they found no explanation.

pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.00052; Kruskal-Wallis p=0.047; CADD 0.63; none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: rs12894869:C (a p53 binding site allele) was more frequent in brown-eyed individuals, again supporting a p53-pigmentation link.
author_explanation_quote: the reference allele of rs12894869, rs12894869:C, with a functional p53 binding site was observed at a higher frequency in the individuals with brown eye colour. This further supports the hypothesis that p53 may promote pigmentation, and may explain the effect of rs12894869 on eye colour
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the SLC24A4 target region)

pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.0053; Kruskal-Wallis p=0.0067; none survived Bonferroni
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; checked for regulatory annotation, found none -- variant-mechanism gap.
author_explanation_quote: The importance of rs1408799 was confirmed in this study. Although not located in a regulatory region, rs1408799 showed association with eye colours
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYRP1 target region)
notes: Authors explicitly checked for regulatory annotation and found none; association confirmed but no mechanism proposed.

locus_id: TYR region LD cluster (chr11 ~88.5-89.0 Mb), representative rs17184781
rsid: rs17184781 (representative of ~96 TYR variants; 18 TYR tag-SNPs)
pvalue: rep Fisher raw p=0.003, KW p=0.00093; region-wide none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: Most associated TYR variants had alternative alleles more frequent in blue-eyed individuals.
author_explanation_quote: Only two of the 39 identified variants, rs7120151 and rs2648640, did not follow this trend...Neither of the SNPs were predicted to be located in regulatory regions nor to have regulatory effects
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYR target region)
notes: rs17184781 tags rs7120151 in Table 3; this sub-cluster explicitly lacks a regulatory annotation or proposed mechanism.

locus_id: SLC24A4 region LD cluster (chr14 ~92.85-92.94 Mb), representative rs10594259
rsid: rs10594259 (representative of 65 SLC24A4 variants; 8 SLC24A4 tag-SNPs)
pvalue: rep Fisher raw p=0.00052, KW p=0.047; region-wide none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: 65 variants in SLC24A4, 51 in TYR, and 39 in TYRP1 were associated with both categorical and quantitative eye colours
classification_notes: SLC24A4 canonical; 'The exact function of SLC24A4 in melanogenesis is unknown' -- variant/gene-mechanism gap on a textbook effector, not effector-uncertain (SLC24A4 itself is unambiguously canonical/on the task's list even though authors say melanogenic mechanism unknown).
author_explanation_quote: The exact function of SLC24A4 in melanogenesis is unknown. However, SLC24A4 is closely related to another calcium transporter...Although the function of SLC24A4 is not known, variants within the SLC24A4 gene have previously been found to be associated with pigmentation
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the SLC24A4 target region)

locus_id: TYRP1 region LD cluster (chr9 ~12.67-12.71 Mb), representative rs59334502
rsid: rs59334502 (representative of 39 TYRP1 variants; 6 TYRP1 tag-SNPs)
pvalue: rep Fisher raw p=0.0033, KW p=0.0021; region-wide none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: 65 variants in SLC24A4, 51 in TYR, and 39 in TYRP1 were associated with both categorical and quantitative eye colours
author_explanation_quote: 43 variants that were located in the same haploblock and in complete LD (r2 = 1)...One tag-SNP in the TYRP1 target region, rs2762457, was not associated with eye colours, but in strong LD with eight other SNPs associated with eye colours
notes: This representative/tag-SNP block is not individually discussed with a proposed molecular mechanism in the Discussion (unlike rs35866166/rs62538956 which are called out by name from the same TYRP1 haploblock).

locus_id: IRF4 region LD cluster (chr6 ~0.38-0.42 Mb), representative rs12211228
rsid: rs12211228 (representative of 11 IRF4 variants; 4 IRF4 tag-SNPs)
coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 chr6:380,958-421,773 (targeted region); rep rs12211228 at 6:408,833
pvalue: rep Fisher raw p=0.038, KW p=0.315 (categorical-only); region-wide none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: Associated IRF4 variants were tied to categorical (not quantitative) eye colour.
classification_notes: IRF4 canonical; p53 TF binding site.
author_explanation_quote: rs12211228:C was predicted to disrupt a transcription factor binding site for the tumor suppressor p53 that was previously reported to be linked to skin pigmentation and shown to promote pigmentation
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the IRF4 target region)

locus_id: SLC45A2 region (chr5 ~33.92-33.99 Mb) — no association; rs16891982 near-fixed
evidence_quote: SLC45A2 rs16891982 (an IrisPlex SNP and ancestry-informative marker) was near-fixed (39/40 individuals GG), so its association could not be detected in Europeans only.
classification_notes: SLC45A2 canonical; near-fixed allele, null result explained by reduced power.
author_explanation_quote: This was expected as rs16891982:G is close to fixation in Europeans (frequency 0.96)...the association with eye colour may be absent in our study because we only investigated Europeans
notes: Authors give an explicit explanation for the NULL result (near-fixation removes power in a Europeans-only sample), which is itself a mechanistic/statistical account of the absence of signal.

evidence_quote: Notably, no variant in SLC45A2, SLC24A5, or the OCA2-HERC2 target regions was associated with eye colour.
classification_notes: SLC24A5 canonical; null result in highly selected dataset.
author_explanation_quote: The lack of association between eye colour and variants in the SLC45A2 and SLC24A5 target regions may also be due to the highly selected dataset

locus_id: OCA2-HERC2 region (chr15 ~28.34-28.37 Mb) — no association; rs12913832 haploblock
rsid: rs12913832 (anchor SNP, fixed GG by design)
evidence_quote: the variants there comprised one large haploblock in strong LD with rs12913832, so most variation was eliminated by the study design, and additional variants there could not explain brown eye colour formation.
classification_notes: OCA2 and HERC2 both canonical; null result, haploblock eliminated by study design.
author_explanation_quote: the variants identified in the OCA2-HERC2 region comprised one large haploblock in strong LD with rs12913832. Hence, most of the variation within this region was eliminated due to the nature of the dataset

coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 chr6 (IRF4 region; IrisPlex predictive SNP)
evidence_quote: the IrisPlex IRF4 SNP rs12203592 showed no association here.
classification_notes: IRF4 canonical; IrisPlex panel, no association found but mechanism restated from literature.
author_explanation_quote: IRF4 has previously been shown to activate the expression of TYR through cooperation with the microphthalmia-associated transcription factor (MITF)...One IRF4 variant, rs12203592, was shown to affect IRF4 expression in melanocytes
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the IRF4 target region)
notes: Paper restates the established IRF4/MITF/TYR mechanism for this marker even though it found NO association in this cohort ('we found no association between rs12203592 and eye colours').

locus_id: SLC24A4 rs12896399 (IrisPlex marker) — did not meet inclusion
coord_build: GRCh37/hg19 chr14 (SLC24A4 region; IrisPlex predictive SNP)
pvalue: did not meet inclusion criteria (raw p>0.05 combined)
evidence_quote: The IrisPlex SLC24A4 SNP rs12896399:T (blue-associated) was more frequent in blue-eyed individuals here but did not meet inclusion criteria.
classification_notes: SLC24A4 canonical; IrisPlex panel, did not meet inclusion criteria.
author_explanation_quote: The exact function of SLC24A4 in melanogenesis is unknown...but the variant did not meet the inclusion criteria
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the SLC24A4 target region)

locus_id: TYR rs1393350 (IrisPlex marker) — LD tag for candidate rs1126809
pvalue: Fisher raw p=0.012, KW p=0.017; CADD 1.9; none survived Bonferroni
evidence_quote: rs1126809 had the highest CADD (29.4) of all variants and is in LD (r2 = 0.84) with the IrisPlex SNP rs1393350 (CADD 1.9); the authors suggest rs1126809 (rather than rs1393350) be used in future prediction models.
classification_notes: TYR canonical; IrisPlex panel, LD with rs1126809 hypothesized r2=0.84.
author_explanation_quote: rs1393350 had a CADD Phred score of only 1.9...and does not have a known regulatory effect...we hypothesize that the association between rs1393350 and eye colour is due to LD with rs1126809
author_explanation_section: Discussion (Variants in the TYR target region)
